sessions.title: Agent/Status Color Tokens in Templates
planned_tasks: Replace ~45 hardcoded agent/status hex colors in template style= attributes
Use hexToRgba() + getAgentColor() or CSS var(--agent-*-primary) tokens
Focus on ProductDetailsDialog, MessageItem, RecentMemoriesList, BroadcastPanel, RoleBadge
Extract repeated patterns to scoped CSS classes
tasks_completed: ProductDetailsDialog: 5 static style= attributes → :style bindings using hexToRgba + getAgentColor/getStatusColor tokens
MessageItem: senderHex, PRIORITY_COLORS, STATUS_COLORS replaced with getAgentColor()/getStatusColor() calls
RecentMemoriesList: typeColors map replaced with getAgentColor() + hexToRgba (removed hardcoded rgba strings)
BroadcastPanel: STATUS_COLORS replaced with token calls; .recipient-chip CSS uses $color-agent-implementor SCSS var
RoleBadge: removed local hexToRgb function, replaced with imported hexToRgba; ROLE_COLORS use getAgentColor()
Full Vitest suite: 99 files, 1916 tests — all passing
deviations: BroadcastPanel had a local getStatusColor function conflicting with the imported getStatusColor from statusConfig — renamed local to getStatusVuetifyColor to avoid clash
sessions.notes_for_next: All agent/status hex colors in templates are now tokenized. Remaining inline styles are layout (display:flex, gap, white-space, max-height) and data-driven :style bindings — those are 0874b scope.
sessions.summary: Replaced hardcoded hex colors across 5 components with centralized token utilities (hexToRgba, getAgentColor, getStatusColor). Zero visual change. All 1916 tests pass.
